response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer, but his background is centered on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the deep, current C++ expertise this role requires. His only professional C++ work was a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and he shows no exposure to industrial automation, MQTT/IoT protocols, message brokers at the architectural level required, or IEC 62443 secure development practices. While his DLang low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby demonstrates transferable systems-programming skills, the gap to the core requirements is too large for a senior C++ leadership position.
strengths: Strong systems-programming foundation from low-latency, high-throughput DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second
Solid engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
Broad multi-domain experience (web, mobile, desktop, embedded) with proven ownership and delivery
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience — roughly six months in 2014–2015, none recent
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, misaligned with an industrial C++ architecture role

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent C++ and STL expertise
No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 exposure
